dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4560 IS: Moving Away from Risk to Key Export Targets Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Ricketts introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry

form.official-title: To direct the Secretary of Agriculture to publish, on an annual basis, an assessment of United States dependency on the exportation of agricultural commodities to adversarial countries, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Moving Away from Risk to Key Export Targets Act of 2026 or the MARKET Act of 2026.

section.header: Agricultural commodities export assessments and reports

subparagraph.text: The term covered export means any agricultural commodity exported by the United States.
any foreign adversary (as defined in section 8(c) of the Secure and Trusted Communications Networks Act of 2019 (47 U.S.C. 1607(c))); and
any foreign country identified by the Secretary pursuant to subsection (d).

paragraph.text: The term nonadversarial, with respect to a foreign country or a market, means, respectively—

subparagraph.text: that the foreign country is not a foreign adversary; and
that the market is located outside the jurisdiction of a foreign adversary.

paragraph.text: The term Secretary means the Secretary of Agriculture.

subsection.text: On an annual basis, the Secretary, in coordination with the United States Trade Representative, shall submit to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate and the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives a report that—

paragraph.text: identifies risks and vulnerabilities with respect to the existing access of the United States to covered export markets of foreign adversaries that pose a risk of exploiting such a market; and
contains an assessment of alternative markets to the markets described in paragraph (1).
address the current and potential market access for each covered export that could be at risk of exploitation by a foreign adversary in the event of a trade disruption or military conflict;

contain recommendations of the Secretary, in coordination with the United States Trade Representative, the Secretary of Commerce, and the Secretary of State, to reduce the dependency of the United States on markets of foreign adversaries for covered exports;
identify alternative markets for increasing covered exports to nonadversarial countries; and

subparagraph.text: describe, monitor changes over time in, and otherwise address potential risks posed by foreign adversaries to covered exports in nonadversarial markets; and

clause.text: methods to address the risks described in subparagraph (A); and

the presence and activities of the United States, unrelated to covered exports, in nonadversarial countries for the purpose of understanding how covered export market and nonmarket presence and activities may limit or constrain the United States with respect to covered exports and related market access.

subsection.text: At the discretion of the Secretary, for purposes of inclusion in an assessment under this section, the Secretary may identify as a foreign adversary any foreign country that the Secretary determines has engaged in a long-term pattern, or 1 or more serious instances, of conduct significantly adverse to—

paragraph.text: the national security of the United States; or
the security and safety of residents of the United States.

subsection.header: Collection, distribution, and protection of information

paragraph.text: The Secretary may collect information as part of an assessment under this section, subject to the condition that any such collection from a private entity shall be conducted on a voluntary basis.

The Secretary, an officer or employee of the Department of Agriculture (or any agency thereof), or any other person shall not disclose to the public any information provided by a private entity for purposes of an assessment under this section, unless the information has been transformed into a statistical or aggregate form that does not allow the identification of the person that supplied any particular information.
The Secretary shall ensure that no report submitted under this section includes any information that is a trade secret or confidential information subject to—

subparagraph.text: section 552(b)(4) of title 5, United States Code; or
section 1905 of title 18, United States Code.

paragraph.text: Information provided to the Secretary as part of an assessment under this section—

subparagraph.text: shall be considered to be specifically exempted from disclosure under section 552(b)(3) of title 5, United States Code; and
shall not be used by the Secretary for any purpose other than to carry out this section.

subsection.text: The Secretary shall terminate the preparation and submission of the report under this section on making a determination that all relevant covered export markets have become sufficiently diversified.
